questions.q: Which Confederate general was known as 'Stonewall' for his defensive tactics?

choices: Slavery was banned in the North only
All enslaved people were freed immediately everywhere
Enslaved people in rebel states were declared free
All enslaved people would be freed after the war

choices: Capture the Confederate capital at Atlanta
Defend against Lee's invasion of the North

questions.q: Which battle in 1862 resulted in the highest number of casualties in a single day in American military history?

choices: To comply with international trade agreements
To support their agricultural economy, particularly cotton production
To defend against Northern military aggression

questions.q: In what year did the Civil War officially end?

To blockade Southern ports and control the Mississippi River to strangle the economy
To invade the South through multiple points simultaneously
To capture Richmond in the first year of the war

It forced Lee to retreat from Virginia
It gave the Union control of Tennessee's rivers and provided its first major victory in the Western Theater
It ended the Confederate threat to Washington D.C.

choices: To gain access to western territories

It boosted Northern morale and helped Lincoln's reelection
It convinced Britain to recognize the Confederacy

To cut off the South's supply of goods and revenue
To prevent Confederate generals from escaping

What document did Lincoln issue on January 1, 1863, freeing slaves in Confederate states?

choices: Plantation agriculture based on enslaved labor

questions.q: Which battle in September 1862 gave the Union its first major victory in the Eastern Theater?

Who was elected president of the United States in 1860, prompting Southern secession?

choices: To establish agriculture and mechanic colleges

questions.q: Which general commanded Confederate forces at the Battle of Fort Sumter, the war's opening engagement?

choices: To grant free land to settlers in the West

questions.q: Which Confederate state was the last to join the Confederacy, doing so only after Fort Sumter was attacked?

Which Confederate general famously said he opposed secession but felt compelled to fight for his home state?

choices: To abolish slavery throughout the nation

The battle that directly freed all enslaved people in the South
The largest cavalry engagement of the war
The bloodiest single day in American military history

To generate revenue specifically for enslaved people reparations

To encourage Southern states to remain in the Union

choices: The last major Confederate blockade-running port

Confederate states in rebellion against the Union

The District of Columbia and border states only

choices: Resulted in a clear Confederate victory

Military alliances formed before the war

questions.q: The CSS Hunley, an experimental Confederate submarine, became historically significant for being the first submarine to do what in combat?
choices: Sink an enemy warship in battle
